DNA exists in the form of __________ str.file_name: DNA exists in the form of __________ strands of DNA coiled about each other..md
DNA exists in the form of __________ str.file_path: /home/araycove/Documents/Education/Question Notes/Biology/DNA exists in the form of __________ strands of DNA coiled about each other..md
subject: biology
DNA exists in the form of __________ str.question_text: DNA exists in the form of __________ strands of DNA coiled about each other.
DNA exists in the form of __________ str.answer_text:
Double 

DNA is made of two strands of nucleotides (basic units of DNA). The DNA molecule looks like a twisted ladder and is referred to as a double helix.  Each nucleotide contains a sugar molecule and a phosphate group, along with a nitrogen base. The sugar molecules and phosphate groups make up the sides of the ladder, and the nitrogen bases make up the “steps” of the ladder.
DNA exists in the form of __________ str.id: DNA exists in the form of __________ strands of DNA coiled about each other..md

The functional unit of heredity is the _.file_name: The functional unit of heredity is the _____________..md
The functional unit of heredity is the _.file_path: /home/araycove/Documents/Education/Question Notes/Biology/The functional unit of heredity is the _____________..md
subject: biology
The functional unit of heredity is the _.question_text: The functional unit of heredity is the _____________.
The functional unit of heredity is the _.answer_text:
The Gene

A gene is the basic unit of heredity. Genes are made of DNA sequences and act as instructions for making proteins (although not all genes make proteins). Genes are found on chromosomes. Chromosomes are located in a cell's nucleus.
The functional unit of heredity is the _.id: The functional unit of heredity is the _____________..md

subject: astronomy
Each element is defined by what?.md.question_text: Each element is defined by what?
Each element is defined by what?.md.answer_text:
Each element is defined by the number of protons in its atoms:

Thus, any atom with 6 protons in its nucleus is called carbon, any with 50 protons is called tin, and any with 70 protons is called ytterbium

Why do we have 7 days in a week?.md.file_name: Why do we have 7 days in a week?.md
Why do we have 7 days in a week?.md.file_path: /home/araycove/Documents/Education/Question Notes/History/Why do we have 7 days in a week?.md
subject: history
astronomy
Why do we have 7 days in a week?.md.question_text: Why do we have 7 days in a week?
Why do we have 7 days in a week?.md.answer_text:
The week was dedicated after the seven objects that move on their own; that's why there are 7 days in week. 

(These seven objects are: Mercury, Venus, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, and the moon - all of which can be seen with the naked eye.)
Why do we have 7 days in a week?.md.id: Why do we have 7 days in a week?.md
